dialog.stepLabel: Elder: "...The Weiqi pieces were scattered, but the guru remained ca..."

items.speaker: Elder
items.text: ...The Weiqi pieces were scattered, but the guru remained calm. "It's getting late," he said gently to the woodcutter. "Shouldn't you be heading down the mountain?"

items.speaker: Elder
items.text: As he glanced at the sky, the woodcutter was shocked to see how much time had passed. "It couldn't have been more than two hours," he thought in disbelief. Feeling apologetic, he prepared to leave, worried that the guru would be annoyed with him.

items.speaker: Elder
items.text: But when the woodcutter turned around, he nearly jumped out of his skin. The hermit guru, who had been playing Weiqi and drinking wine, was now gone. In his place lay a shattered Weiqi board and a heap of decaying bones.

items.speaker: Elder
items.text: The woodcutter was so scared that he dropped his bamboo basket and ran down the mountain without even looking back.

items.speaker: Elder
items.text: As he reached the mountain's base, he was hit with a stark realization. The quaint village he remembered had bloomed into a bustling town, with sturdy brick houses replacing the humble thatched cottages. And where his home once stood, only an empty wasteland remained.

items.speaker: Elder
items.text: He noticed a date on the calendar that he had never seen before. In shock, he grabbed a passerby and urgently asked, "Excuse me, can you tell me the current date and time?"

items.speaker: Elder
items.text: As he heard the answer, his legs went weak. Everything had changed—time had passed and his loved ones were gone. No one remembered him anymore.

items.speaker: Perplexed Audience
items.text: So the woodcutter left Mt. Firmament, but how was he still alive and kicking? With a hundred year's worth of time inside him, shouldn't he...

items.speaker: Onlookers
items.text: Your story... It's a bit far-fetched, don't you think? I mean, if I wasn't from Hongzhen, I might have fallen for it.

items.speaker: Elder
items.text: ...Well! That's the whole point. The truth is stranger than fiction, my friend. Every detail in that story is based on real events.

items.speaker: Elder
items.text: Let me share with you the other legend of Mt. Firmament, for deep within its sacred peaks lies a powerful secret... the "Secret of Immortality"!

items.speaker: Elder
items.text: We all know that Mt. Firmament is the abode of the Sentinel, gathering spiritual energies from both heaven and earth for countless eons. Over time, even the mountain itself has taken on the form of our majestic Sentinel!

items.speaker: Elder
items.text: Living in the mountains for long stretches of time, surrounded by fresh air and drinking from nature's own bounty, can add years to one's life span!

items.speaker: Elder
items.text: It all starts with that game of Weiqi, one that the woodcutter stumbled upon. Too bad those secrets have been long forgotten...

items.speaker: Elder
items.text: Gather around now, people. You're in for the story of a lifetime...

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Shh...

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: ...I'll see you there.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: *yawn* What's with the ruckus... Huh, what’s that in your hands?

options.text: Changli gave it to me a moment ago.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Hmm... "Behold Hongzhen in its entirety, next to a ring and hanging eaves. When eternal snow thaw into flowing streams, let us reconvene."

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Ooh... I see, I see...

options.text: Do you know what it means?
Ooh... So that's how it is...

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: I see I've got no clue at all!

options.text: So much for the anticipation!
Wow, I'm so shocked to hear that.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: I get the very last part, at least! She’s asking you out! Well that's a fancy way to write stuff...

options.text: ...The first part reads like it's about the meeting spot.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: And now... this next sentence, "eternal snow thaw into streams"... What is that supposed to mean?

options.text: Maybe it has something to do with local folklore.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: How did you manage to get all that hidden meaning?

options.text: I'm experienced. Dealt with Jinhsi's riddles already.
Thanks to my big brain.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: But the buildings in Hongzhen all look pretty much the same. Which one exactly was she talking about?

options.text: We can ask around to find out. I’ve done this before.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Makes sense. Lead the way!

dialog.stepLabel: Abby: "Hey. Why are the people here so divided about Changli?..."

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Hey. Why are the people here so divided about Changli?

options.text: She doesn't seem to have ill intents.
We'll have to figure it out ourselves.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Hmm... You humans make everything so complicated.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Well, at least now we know what she wanted to say.

options.text: "Let’s meet at the highest place in Hongzhen, in the early afternoon."

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Now that’s much easier to understand. Why did she have to make it a whole riddle? *yawn*... I’m getting sleepy again.

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: Rover?

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: Nice to see you again. How are you liking Hongzhen so far?

options.text: It's nice. The place is bustling with life.
It's much more lively than before.

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: The crisis at Mt. Firmament has been resolved, and the waterway between Jinzhou and Hongzhen is open once again. As a result, there has been an increase in visitors from Jinzhou, making the town more lively than before.

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: With the chaos subsiding, our town and city are now reconnected, allowing residents to live and work in peace.

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: We owe you a debt of gratitude, Rover, for your efforts in clearing the remaining effects of Temporal Disruptions in Mt. Firmament over the past few days.

options.text: Just doing my part.
Couldn't have done it without everyone.
That job was a beast. You better have my money ready.

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: *chuckle* Of course.

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: We're all indebted to you, Hongzhen and its people alike. If there's anything you need, don't hesitate to ask.

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: I couldn't help but notice you've been asking around. Is there something you need assistance with?

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: (Xinyi and Changli have a mutual trust as acquaintances. It should be fine to ask Xinyi instead...)

options.text: Well...

items.text: You tell Xinyi about your encounter with Changli and what the passersby said about her.

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: Hmm... I see. That sounds like something Changli would do.

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: Even though we're not that close, I know a bit about her personality. She wouldn't just hand over something that symbolizes her identity to anyone, especially when...

options.text: Especially what?

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: I think it would be better for her to tell you personally.

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: The Magistrate's Counselor is shrouded in mystery, and her motives are often hard to decipher...

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: When she wants something done, it's never as straightforward as it seems, and few can grasp the true meaning behind her plans.

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: The rumors and controversies surrounding her never cease, but she seems unfazed.

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: However... I believe one has to get involved personally to truly understand Changli's intentions, regardless of public opinion.

options.text: My plans exactly.
I'm very curious about her plans.

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: About the riddle... it seems you have already figured it out by yourself.

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: But remember, it's not just about the answer, but also what she wants you to understand.

options.text: Indeed.

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: *chuckle* Then... If you'll excuse me.

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: If there's anything else you need here in Hongzhen, please do not hesitate to let me know.

options.text: Thank you.

dialog.stepLabel: Rover: "(A ring, she wrote... Should be this ring-shaped ancient str..."

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (A ring, she wrote... Should be this ring-shaped ancient structure, I guess?)

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Wow, it’s really huge now that we look at it.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Next, hanging eaves... If we look from here... Is it that pavilion?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: It does look quiet. Should be a nice place for meetings... *yawn* Ugh, getting 'eepy again…

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: That must be the place, then.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I’ll get going.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Apologies for my delay.

options.text: It's fine. I just got here too.
Something tells me you've been here for a while.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Thank you for accepting my invitation.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: You looked adorable, focusing on the Weiqi board. I didn't want to interrupt you.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Thank you for accepting my invitation.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Oh? And what makes you think so?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Those tokens from Jinhsi... It was your idea, was it not?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: *chuckle*...So you've guessed it.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Sorry, {PlayerName}. Jinzhou faced multiple threats back then, and we had to be cautious. We did not intend to inconvenience you. I regret our lack of hospitality.

options.text: It's okay. Jinhsi had explained your reasons.
I'm more or less used to it by now...
It's a thing of the past.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Still, I owe you an apology in person.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: This time... *chuckle* I just wanted to see you. The brilliant mind that always understood my unspoken words.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: ...I am very grateful you decided to come.

options.text: What's the deal this time? Another Tacet Discord to beat?
What's the deal this time? Another errand to run?
Let's cut to the chase. What do you want from me?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Apologies for giving you that impression, Rover. That’s not how we treat our saviors.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Apologies for giving you that impression, Rover. That’s not how we treat our saviors.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: I merely have a few questions, and I was wondering if you may have the answers.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Does this game of <ano=Go>Weiqi</ano> look familiar to you, Rover?

options.text: No.
...I'm not sure.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: ...I see.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: ...Apologies. I heard about your memory loss. Please pardon me.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: There is a local tale about the "Secret of Immortality" being hidden on Mt. Firmament. Have you heard about it?

options.text: The one I heard in Hongzhen?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Yes. I have a slightly different version of this tale to share.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Centuries ago, a Taoist cultivator met an enigmatic visitor on Mt. Firmament. Despite their age difference, they soon became friends and often played Weiqi together.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: One day, a woodcutter stumbled upon them, having lost his way home. Their game mesmerized him so much that he didn't notice the red tie on his basket rotting away… Panicked, he bumped into the Weiqi board.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Despite the interruption, the visitor remained unfazed. They offered the woodcutter some tea, then guided him down the mountain, shielding him from dangerous Temporal Disruptions. After that, the visitor disappeared.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Many years later, the Taoist cultivator became a legendary hermit guru. Upon returning to Mt. Firmament in his final years, he reunited with that elusive visitor.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: The two resumed their unfinished game. People believe the visitor shared their Secret of Immortality through cleverly-placed Weiqi pieces, as a final parting gift.

options.text: You mean this game in front of us?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Yes... and no. This game is missing the final twenty moves. It is unfinished.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: That was a bit different from what you heard before, I guess? The story changed over time, infused with fantasy in each retelling.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: But that's how old tales go, slowly losing their truth as they're passed down.

options.text: You mean the other folklores may hold some truth too.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Precisely.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: The legend may be shrouded in myth, but that game of Weiqi was real. Embedded within its records lies a cryptic message: "This game record can solve the crisis of time."

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: From what I know, only the Sentinel in its prime has the power to manipulate time through its possession of a complete Temporal Mandate. For us mere mortals, controlling time is indeed similar to gaining immortality.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: As the records state, it only reveals itself when Mt. Firmament emerges. But how it ties to the Temporal Mandate remains an enigma... at least for now.

options.text: The Sentinel's wounds...
There might be an undamaged sample of Temporal Mandate.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Yes. We may find a way to heal them.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: If that is the case, we’ll be able to heal the Sentinel's wounds.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: But... our chances are looking slim. That game record could just hold my teacher's memories, not any real value.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: So I am presenting this as merely a personal request.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: I am planning to go find that record. Could you please join me?

options.text: Sure.
Why me?
I’ll consider it, but what's in it for me?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Thank you for lending me a hand. I promise it will be worth your while.

options.text: I am interested in this tale too.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: As we have been to Mt. Firmament before, I imagined it would pique your interest, as the tale concerns our Sentinel.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: And, moreover... You are special to me, and… to Mt. Firmament. I hope I have provided enough reasons to clear your doubts.

options.text: Special?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: I asked you about the unfinished Weiqi game, because I believe it may have something to do with your past.

options.text: You mean...

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Yes… After all, it takes two to play Weiqi, right?

options.text: I see. Then I suppose I have no reason to not go.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: If my speculation is correct… That game of Weiqi may hold clues to your lost memories.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: In addition… I promise to aid you in any way I can after we return with the record. How does that sound?

options.text: I see. Then I have no reason to not go.
Alright. Count me in!

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Splendid.

options.text: It's still early. How about we set off now?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Very well. Let's meet on the gallery road outside Hongzhen.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: I still have some preparations to do. Please go ahead, I'll join you later.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Rover, you're here.

options.text: Wasn't expecting you to arrive before me.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Well... As the Wayfinder, I do know a couple shortcuts.

options.text: I trust you’ll guide me well, Miss Wayfinder.
Where are we going?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: The notes only provided a vague idea of where the game record is. I've determined the most likely location on my last visit.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: We’ll be heading there. See if we can find anything new.

options.text: You've been there before?
That’s surprisingly straightforward, especially coming from you.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: After dealing with those Artificers, I ruled out the other locations on my way to join you.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: But one can only do so much alone. This is why I need your help.

options.text: Lucky for you, I'm very good at finding things.
Gotta throw myself a challenge from time to time.
I'm not planning to go back on my own words.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: How reliable.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Then let's get going.

dialog.stepLabel: Changli: "We can see the whole of Hongzhen from here...."

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: We can see the whole of Hongzhen from here.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: I never noticed how beautiful it looked during my previous visits.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: It's the same place as always. I suppose it's my state of mind that has changed.

options.text: How did it change?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: I don't know. Maybe it's because... Your presence makes me feel at ease.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Wait... I smell Tacet Discords in that cave!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: No... There's more... Tacet Discords, humans, and...

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Smells like a whole bunch of messed up frequencies... It's so... familiar...

options.text: (How could there be any Tacet Discords in Mt. Firmament?)
(Is Abby sleep talking again?)

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: C’mon, after all we've been through, you still don't trust my sniffer? Be grateful, I'm giving you a warning!

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Rover? Did something happen?

options.text: Tacet Discords could be assaulting people over there.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: It's in the same direction we’re going. Could it be...

options.text: Let's go take a look.

items.speaker: Explorer
items.text: T-Thanks for your help...

options.text: Are you all right?

items.speaker: Explorer
items.text: I... I'm okay. Wait, you... Are you THE Rover...?

options.text: How do you know me?

items.speaker: Explorer
items.text: You're that hero who fought against the Threnodian, and you also resolved the time crisis here with Madam Magistrate. Everyone's been talking about you...

items.speaker: Explorer
items.text: And Lady Changli... I-I never thought I'd have the chance to see you in person...

items.speaker: Explorer
items.text: ...Without your help, I would've been a goner for sure...

items.speaker: Explorer
items.text: I can't die here. Not when I've got someone waiting for me...

items.speaker: Explorer
items.text: I've finally, finally found it... After all this time... Ugh...

options.text: Take it easy. Everything's all right.
Relax. Your wounds look pretty bad.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: While we've solved the crisis in Mt. Firmament for the time being... Public access to the mountain is still restricted. Before entering the mountain, any explorer must submit a request and can only do so while accompanied by the Midnight Rangers.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Sir, could you please share your reasons for traveling here alone?

items.speaker: Explorer
items.text: I, I heard about Mt. Firmament's legend... So I came to find its secret...

items.speaker: Explorer
items.text: I've only been in the mountain for half a day, everything was going smoothly before those monsters pounced on me on my way back... *cough*...

items.speaker: Explorer
items.text: I must get back... Fuling... She's still waiting for me...

options.text: He doesn't look like he's only been here for half a day...
He seems a bit off.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: It seems he is still hiding something from us.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Some of those injuries are older than half a day...

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: He needs to go back to Hongzhen for proper medical attention... Time is against us.

options.text: We’ll need to escort him down the mountain.
Something feels strange about this place.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Agreed.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: The appearance of Tacet Discords is indeed unusual… We can ask him later when he's recovered a bit more.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Indeed. Tacet Discords appearing in such numbers on Mt. Firmament, of all places… This is unusual, to say the least.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: We can continue our conversation later, after getting him to safety.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: On that note, we still don't know his identity... Judging from this uniform, he might be affiliated with the Pioneer Association.

options.text: The Association has a presence in Hongzhen right now.
He mentioned someone waiting for him.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Then perhaps that "someone" is in Hongzhen. Could be his friend or an acquaintance.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Good point, Rover. Let's head back.
You take the adventurer in coma and return to Hongzhen with Changli

items.speaker: Young Woman
items.text: W-Wuli! I.. *cough*...

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Breathe. Take it easy.

items.speaker: Young Woman
items.text: *cough*... Thank you. I'm okay now. Who are you? ... And Wuli, is he...

options.text: You must be Fuling?

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: Yes, I am... Can you please tell me what happened?

items.speaker: Wuli
items.text: Fuling...!

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: Wuli... You're hurt?! I've been looking for you... You didn't pick up my calls...

items.speaker: Wuli
items.text: Great news, Fuling! ...I found it! Finally!

items.speaker: Wuli
items.text: I've found a way to slow down your illness. No more pain, my love... We can finally be together, forever!

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: You mean... that legend? It's real?

items.speaker: Wuli
items.text: Fuling... Are you... Are you not happy?

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: No... I am... I'm so glad to hear it.

items.speaker: Wuli
items.text: Haha... Yes...

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: ...Wuli!
You carry an exhausted and unconscious Wuli to a doctor to treat his injuries.

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: Thank you, {PlayerName}, Lady Changli. You saved his life...

options.text: I'm glad he's okay.

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: May I please ask... what on earth happened? I've never seen Wuli like that...

options.text: Well...

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: So... He said he only spent half a day in the mountains?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Was that not the case?

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: No... I've been... I've been waiting for almost a week.

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: It's not unusual for Wuli to be out of touch for a few days when he goes on long trips. This time, however, seemed longer than usual. If it weren't for you two showing up, I would have asked the Midnight Rangers to help me search for him in the mountains.

options.text: What's his obsession with the mountains?

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: Wuli, he... He did it for me.

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: I used to work as a landscape painter. Wuli and I met through work, and we traveled to many places together.

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: A few months ago, I got really sick. It affected my ability to walk, which got worse over time. We couldn't go on our adventures like we used to. Wuli stopped taking on work and traveled all over the world to find a cure for me.

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: Despite his efforts, we couldn't find any cures... Until two weeks ago, when he brought me to Hongzhen. Said he had a surprise for me.

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: I thought it was just going to be a relaxing trip, but he disappeared for seven days. Now he's back, rambling about having found a way to stop my condition from progressing...

options.text: He was saying something about the "legend"...

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Mt. Firmament's "Secret of Immortality", I presume?

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: Yes... Wuli told me that he's researched and found an untouched paradise on Mt. Firmament. A place where time stands still. He said if I lived there, my condition would never worsen...

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: ...It cannot fully cure me, but at least we can spend eternity together.

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: At first, I thought it was just a myth... But then you brought up how he only spent half a day in the mountains...

options.text: He may have run into Temporal Disruptions.

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: Those were my thoughts as well, but Wuli seemed very certain.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Mt. Firmament used to have Temporal Disruptions caused by its tumultuous history. However, thanks to Magistrate Jinhsi and Rover, these disruptions have been dealt with. Or so we had hoped.

options.text: Looks like there might still be some of them.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: So there are still unaddressed Temporal Disruptions in the mountains. And unfortunately, Wuli has stumbled upon one, mistaking it for the legendary cure he was searching for.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: But little did he know, it would only put him in more danger.

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: I see...

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: ...So that's why there were rumors of Rangers being here to take care of the leftover Tacet Discords, and block off access to the mountain...

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: Then... if this Temporal Disruption you speak of is truly so dangerous...

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: {PlayerName}, Lady Changli, please help us get rid of it. For the sake of everyone here.

options.text: But your friend...

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: He truly thinks it's the only way to save you. He's willing to take a slim chance, just for you.

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: Freezing my own time... I don't think I should do that. It's not my best option. I appreciate your concerns, but I'll be fine.

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: Dreams always come to an end. Eventually we have to wake up, and keep moving forward.

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: *cough* I... I'm okay. I haven't talked this much in a long time.

options.text: I'll go get a doctor for you.
You need to rest.

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: ...I'm just a little tired. I'll be fine after some rest.

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: Once Wuli wakes up, I'll explain everything to him.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: If you have any other ideas, just tell him.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: But if you keep it all to yourself... No one, even those closest to you, will know how you really feel.

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: ...Yes. I understand.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: {PlayerName}, things are taking a turn. We should probably tell Lady Xinyi first.

options.text: Indeed. Let's go.

dialog.stepLabel: Changli: "Now it has become quite clear what the two were thinking...."

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Now it has become quite clear what the two were thinking.

options.text: So this is what she had on her mind...
Did you know about it all along?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: I had my speculation.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Fuling has come to terms with her impending death. She doesn't buy into the legends of "immortality." Instead, she simply wants to make the most of the time she has left and fulfill her and Wuli's dreams together.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: If Wuli wants to reach that so-called "paradise," he'll be trapped inside forever. It also means he'll have to give up his entire future as an explorer.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: That is not what she wants for him.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Her ultimate desire is for her lover to embrace her inevitable passing, and to realize the joint aspirations they hold in her place.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: She hasn't given up, but rather clings to hope for their future, even if she won't be there to see it unfold.

options.text: Would you make the same choice... If it had happened to you?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Hmm...? Why the sudden question?

options.text: It seems you don't care that much about your own death.
You seem to understand Fuling deeply.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Is that how you see me? Hmm... Perhaps for some, death is just a destination. There are more significant things to focus on.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: I'm just making an educated guess from what we've collected. And honestly, Fuling isn't exactly good at keeping things to herself.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Let's stay focused on the issue. While the diary is cryptic, it still exposes the true intentions of whoever misled Wuli.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: It's no coincidence. All the clues point to a correlation between the Temporal Disruption and the legends of the Weiqi game. Or maybe... someone's trying to make us connect the dots.

options.text: Wuli's diary mentioned the Sentinel.
Since the Fractsidus already know so much about the Sentinel...

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Yes. Information on the actual powers of Jué... This isn't the kind of information an ordinary explorer can obtain.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: I guess our "old friends" have still not given up.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: They went to great lengths just to lead us here. It would be rather rude of us to not respond in some way.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: According to the information in my master's notes, and Wuli's map...

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: ...The place we're looking for should be just ahead.

dialog.stepLabel: Rover: "Traces of excavation on the ground... Looks like someone was..."

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Traces of excavation on the ground... Looks like someone was trying to dig a hole with their bare hands. Could it be Wuli?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: This is... a Weiqi board.

options.text: Could it have anything to do with the game record?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: The abnormal time flow here is most likely caused by the Chronosorters. Perhaps there is a way to fix it.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: You've come this far and followed us here, yet you hesitate to reveal yourselves. Guess I'll have to take the lead and extend an invitation.

items.speaker: Fractsidus Artificer
items.text: ...Don't assume you'll always be in control, Changli.

options.text: Enough talk.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Our friendly chat is over. It's time to say goodbye.

dialog.stepLabel: Fractsidus Artificer: "The Sonoro Sphere is open. Go!..."

items.speaker: Fractsidus Artificer
items.text: The Sonoro Sphere is open. Go!

options.text: Stop them!

items.speaker: Fractsidus Artificer
items.text: Sorry, but you're not getting past me.

items.speaker: Fractsidus Artificer
items.text: Jué's formidable time-bending abilities, at their peak... All trapped within this Sonoro Sphere, as I'm sure you are well aware.

options.text: It's only a legend.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Do you really want to chase after this legend when you don't even know if it's real?

items.speaker: Fractsidus Artificer
items.text: So what? Wouldn't it be a sight to behold as this Sonoro Sphere consumes everything in its path, while the Temporal Disruptions bring death?

options.text: You're out of your mind...

items.speaker: Fractsidus Artificer
items.text: Anyway... {PlayerName}, thank you for letting us into this Sonoro Sphere.

items.speaker: Fractsidus Artificer
items.text: You are indeed very special, as the Excellency foretold.

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: It's so beautiful...

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: Wuli, this is the miracle we've been waiting to see! It's hope reborn after destruction...

items.speaker: Wuli
items.text: Fuling...

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: It's over.

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: Did you find what you were looking for?

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: Thank you for your help. The Midnight Rangers have successfully apprehended all remaining Fractsidus members in the mountains, and the effects of the Temporal Disruption have now vanished.

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: I lost all contact with you during this whole time. Did you run into any trouble?

options.text: (Tell Xinyi what happened.)

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: Temporal Disruption hidden inside a Sonoro Sphere... It makes sense now why it stayed hidden for so long.

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: I never thought the solution would be to burn down the entire Sonoro Sphere from the inside...

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: You went too far back there, Changli.

options.text: Changli, about your condition...
Yeah, that was a bit too risky.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: I know my limits.

items.speaker: Wuli
items.text: Rover, Lady Changli...

options.text: How are you feeling?

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: Wuli is fine now, thanks to his doctor's help. As for me... Well, at least I can still walk a bit.

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: After all, it's been almost five days...

options.text: So it's already been five days...

items.speaker: Wuli
items.text: I never properly thanked you for bringing me back to Hongzhen. I heard about the attack by the Fractsidus in the mountains. I... I'm so sorry...

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: *chuckle* No need for that. I understand.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: The Fractsidus had their eyes on the same thing as we did. They've been planning an ambush on us for a long time. Why would we blame it on you?

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: We will never forget how you saved us. You gave us the opportunity to witness such a breathtaking sight. Thanks to your help, this journey holds no regrets for me.

items.speaker: Wuli
items.text: As for the truth behind the so-called Secret of Immortality... Fuling has told me everything.

items.speaker: Wuli
items.text: I had a gut feeling it would turn out to be a disappointment, but I couldn't give up. And in the end, I lost sight of why I even started this journey...

items.speaker: Wuli
items.text: ...But this isn't the first time this has happened. Let's just call it another adventure coming to an end.

items.speaker: Wuli
items.text: I've talked about it with Fuling. From now on, I'll take her on more trips to see more new places together.

items.speaker: Wuli
items.text: Until...

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: Until the day... when I can no longer hold a pen. I've already talked to the folks at the publishing house, and they're happy to let me continue painting for them.

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: My eventual departure may be unavoidable, but I want to give it my all until the very end.

options.text: No matter what happens, please take care.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I know you have already looked past death, but remember... There are people who care about you. They want you to live on and be happy.

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: Yes, I understand. Sometimes, I wish...

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: ...we could stay together a little longer, if only for a while...

items.speaker: Wuli
items.text: Of course, Fuling, I... We've always been...

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: It's not time to be sad yet, is it?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: I've compiled a list of renowned doctors in Huanglong. They're highly skilled and may offer assistance for your medical condition, which is challenging but not impossible to cure.

items.speaker: Fuling
items.text: Lady Changli, I truly can't thank you enough...

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: No need for thanks.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Hmm... If you're feeling generous and want to repay me, I hope to see Fuling's work in the next issue of "Artists of Huanglong".

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Everyone's been missing seeing their favorite landscape artist's work lately.

options.text: Didn't know you were a magazine reader.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Are you surprised? Well, now I'm curious. What kind of a person do you see me as?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Now that the situation has been resolved, you should return to Hongzhen. You deserve some good rest.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: After going through so much, you must be exhausted, {PlayerName}.

options.text: What about you?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Me? Well, I think Lady Xinyi could use the extra help with dealing with the aftermath.

options.text: Are you sure you'll be okay?
Got it.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Well, It wouldn't hurt to have a little more faith in me, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Splendid.

items.speaker: Xinyi
items.text: It's getting late. Maybe we should wait until we get back to discuss anything else.

dialog.stepLabel: Changli: "How are you feeling, {PlayerName}? Well-rested, I hope...."

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: How are you feeling, {PlayerName}? Well-rested, I hope.

options.text: I'm feeling great, but...
I'm more concerned about you.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Aww. Can't you see I'm perfectly fine, sitting right in front of you?

options.text: I'm still a little worried.
Is Mt. Firmament back to normal?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Don't worry, {PlayerName}. We are only having a casual conversation today. Nothing taxing.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Besides, you only agreed to join me here for more questions, right?

options.text: That's not my only reason...
Indeed.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Oh, is that so? I'm curious then.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Sure, ask away. I'm happy to answer them to the best of my ability.

options.text: About the game record and "Secret of Immortality"...
About the Sonoro Sphere...
About the little girl in that Sonoro Sphere...

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: It all started a century ago, when my mentor, Master Xuanmiao, had a brief encounter with a certain peculiar visitor.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: During a TD Outbreak, my master was rescued by that individual. {Male=He;Female=She} exuded a unique energy and was always elusive. My master dug through ancient texts and unearthed {Male=his;Female=her} presence in long-forgotten history.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: After searching for several years, he finally met that person on Mt. Firmament again. They played a game of Weiqi together, and that was how the legendary game record came into being.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: At first, I thought my master meant the game record held actual information about the time crisis. But it was just a clue. The real key was finding you, the person behind it all.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: And lastly, the Secret of Immortality... It's most likely just the woodcutter's own spin on the tale.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: My master and the mysterious "visitor" had set a trap with the Sonoro Sphere, using the Weiqi game as a guise. They hid it with the Court of Savantae's Chronosorters, and it would only activate once the time flow on Mt. Firmament was restored to its normal state.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Despite the Sentinel and Mt. Firmament being saved, the Fractsidus would not go down without a fight. They were convinced that the Temporal Mandate was hidden within the game of Weiqi, but little did they know, they had already fallen into a trap.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Pure coincidence or the calculated workings of the Fractsidus, the explorer stumbled upon the Weiqi board, setting off its defense mechanisms.

options.text: I see...

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: The Sentinel was already damaged when it arrived here. My master had foreseen the disasters that would befall Mt. Firmament, so he created this mechanism as a precautionary measure.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: The Sonoro Sphere would devour all who attempted to enter, unless they knew the solution to that puzzle.

options.text: The solution? You mean burning down everything?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Precisely.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: The only way to escape was by burning the Sonoro Sphere without hurting anyone inside. It required masterful control of Forte. Thinking back, it was probably his ultimate test for me.

options.text: It also thwarted the Fractsidus's plans.
Didn't know someone could plan so much ahead...

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: That was his first goal with this Sonoro Sphere.

options.text: There is a second one?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Before leaving, Master entrusted me with a mission: To find that individual... The one who played Weiqi with him. The one who left behind that game record.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: After my master's passing, {Male=he;Female=she} would be the only one who could control the Sonoro Sphere. By now, you should know who {Male=he;Female=she} is.

options.text: Is it me?
I think it's me.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Yes.

options.text: But I don't even remember that Sonoro Sphere.
I see that's why you seemed to know a lot about me.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: That's why you needed me as your Wayfinder.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: My master often mentioned you before his passing.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: And from the moment you arrived in Jinzhou, I had my suspicions about your true identity. Our journey to Mt. Firmament solidified my beliefs.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: And finally, the Weiqi board in that Sonoro Sphere sealed the truth for me.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Indeed... Those memories... They were all real.

options.text: I saw... a little girl in the Sonoro Sphere.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Yes... That's all in the past, just a little homeless girl with no name.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: I never thought we could make new memories in the Sonoro Sphere, where only the past repeats itself.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: A moment of companionship and protection meant everything to me.

options.text: But I wasn't really there for you. Not in the real past.
If there isn't a Secret to Immortality, what about you...

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: I can sense your unspoken question. Did I sacrifice my life when I burned down the Sonoro Sphere, like when I was a child?

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: What I told you there was true. But I've grown and gained control over my powers. Using my Forte now won't harm me in any way. Of that you can rest assured.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Also... If you really are the legendary person who changed the course of history, then you are perhaps the closest to immortality aside from our Sentinel.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: We mortals come and go, our lives are full of meetings and goodbyes. Our time on this earth is fleeting compared to yours. So don't let it bother you.

options.text: I live in the moment, just like you do.
...That is not the same.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: I see. Perhaps this is why... It's so hard to leave this beautiful world behind.

items.speaker: Changli
items.text: Then... Would you be willing to finish that game of Weiqi with me instead?

options.text: Of course.
